decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.3°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective immediate action is to increase the thermal conductivity of the heat spreader to enhance lateral heat spreading and reduce the hotspot intensity. A conservative 40% increase is proposed, staying well within the allowed 2.0x step ratio.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat distribution away from the chip center, directly lowering the peak temperature.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature profile across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if conductivity becomes too high relative to the base plate extraction capacity
Further iterations may require increasing base_material.conductivity if spreader tuning alone is insufficient
